domain_fit.summary: Parameters 40-50 extend TRIZ into modern engineering and technology domains that Altshuller's 1971 patent analysis could not anticipate. They address system-level concerns (security, sustainability, scalability) and quality attributes (aesthetics, controllability, information capacity) that dominate 21st-century product and software design.

40.name: Function efficiency
40.domain_coverage: Systems engineering, lean manufacturing, energy systems, software performance optimization
new_problem_types: Improving energy conversion ratios without increasing system complexity
Optimizing algorithmic efficiency vs resource consumption tradeoffs
Reducing waste in manufacturing processes while maintaining throughput

41.name: Noise/vibration (harmful)
41.domain_coverage: Acoustics, EMI/EMC engineering, signal processing, urban planning, consumer electronics
new_problem_types: Reducing electromagnetic interference in dense electronic assemblies
Minimizing acoustic noise in electric vehicles while maintaining performance
Signal-to-noise ratio optimization in communications systems

42.name: Harmful emissions
42.domain_coverage: Environmental engineering, green chemistry, industrial ecology, carbon capture, regulatory compliance
new_problem_types: Reducing carbon footprint without sacrificing manufacturing capacity
Eliminating toxic byproducts in chemical processes
Managing thermal emissions in data center design

43.name: Compatibility/connectability
43.domain_coverage: Systems integration, API design, IoT interoperability, standards compliance, plug-and-play architectures
new_problem_types: Achieving backward compatibility without constraining new feature development
Integrating legacy systems with modern cloud platforms
Cross-platform interoperability vs platform-specific optimization

44.name: Security/vulnerability
44.domain_coverage: Cybersecurity, physical security, information assurance, privacy engineering, threat modeling
new_problem_types: Hardening system security without degrading usability or performance
Balancing data accessibility with data protection requirements
Securing IoT devices with constrained computational resources

45.name: Safety
45.domain_coverage: Functional safety (IEC 61508), autonomous systems, medical devices, consumer product safety, fail-safe design
new_problem_types: Achieving safety integrity levels without prohibitive cost or complexity
Designing fail-safe modes for autonomous vehicle systems
Balancing safety interlocks with operational efficiency

46.name: Environmental impact/sustainability
46.domain_coverage: Lifecycle assessment, circular economy, ESG compliance, green design, carbon-neutral manufacturing
new_problem_types: Designing for recyclability without compromising structural performance
Reducing lifecycle environmental footprint while keeping unit cost competitive
Sourcing sustainable materials without supply chain disruption

47.name: Amount of information
47.domain_coverage: Data engineering, bandwidth optimization, storage systems, information density, AI/ML data pipelines
new_problem_types: Increasing data throughput without proportional infrastructure scaling
Compressing information without losing critical fidelity
Managing data volume growth in ML training pipelines

48.name: Controllability/responsiveness
48.domain_coverage: Control systems, UX design, real-time systems, adaptive systems, human-machine interfaces
new_problem_types: Achieving sub-millisecond response times without over-provisioning hardware
Improving user control granularity without overwhelming cognitive load
Designing adaptive control for systems with high parameter uncertainty

49.name: Scalability
49.domain_coverage: Cloud architecture, distributed systems, microservices, manufacturing scale-up, organizational design
new_problem_types: Scaling horizontally without introducing coordination overhead or consistency issues
Transitioning from prototype to mass production without redesign
Scaling ML model inference without proportional cost increase

50.name: Aesthetics/appearance
50.domain_coverage: Industrial design, UX/UI, consumer electronics, architecture, branding, sensory engineering
new_problem_types: Improving visual appeal without increasing manufacturing complexity or cost
Balancing minimalist design with functional indicator requirements
Maintaining brand aesthetic across diverse product lines with different constraints

strength_areas.description: The matrix provides contradiction resolution paths for security vs usability, security vs performance, and security vs scalability — tradeoffs that dominate modern system design. No classic TRIZ parameter directly addresses security.
strength_areas.example_contradiction: Improving Security (44) worsens Ease of operation (33): the matrix suggests principles like Segmentation (1), Nesting (7), and Copying (26) — map to micro-segmentation, sandboxing, and security tokens/proxies.

strength_areas.description: Dedicated parameters for harmful emissions and lifecycle sustainability fill a major gap. Classic TRIZ has 'object-generated harmful factors' (31) but lacks explicit environmental lifecycle thinking.
strength_areas.example_contradiction: Improving Environmental impact (46) worsens Weight of moving object (1): principles 8, 19, 23, 25 suggest counterweighting with renewable materials, periodic-action energy recovery, feedback-based optimization, and self-service recycling.

strength_areas.description: Scalability (49) directly targets the defining challenge of cloud-native and distributed systems. Combined with Amount of information (47) and Compatibility (43), it enables contradiction analysis for microservice decomposition, data sharding, and API versioning.
strength_areas.example_contradiction: Improving Scalability (49) worsens Device complexity (36): the matrix suggests Segmentation (1), Nesting (7), Partial action (16), Copying (26) — directly analogous to microservices, containerization, partial replication, and read replicas.

strength_areas.description: Controllability/responsiveness (48) and Aesthetics (50) combined with classic Ease of operation (33) create a richer parameter space for UI/UX contradiction resolution — enabling analysis of control fidelity vs simplicity, and visual appeal vs information density.
strength_areas.example_contradiction: Improving Aesthetics (50) worsens Amount of information (47): explore principles for progressive disclosure, information layering, and context-adaptive display.

strength_areas.description: Safety (45) as a first-class parameter enables direct contradiction modeling for autonomous vehicles, medical devices, and industrial robotics — domains where safety vs performance and safety vs cost are existential tradeoffs.
strength_areas.example_contradiction: Improving Safety (45) worsens Speed (9): principles suggest protective buffering, redundancy, and staged acceleration — mapping to safety envelopes and graceful degradation in autonomous systems.

strength_areas.description: Compatibility/connectability (43) directly addresses interoperability challenges in IoT ecosystems, smart buildings, and industrial automation where heterogeneous systems must cooperate without centralized control.
strength_areas.example_contradiction: Improving Compatibility (43) worsens Security (44): the classic tension between openness and protection in API design, federated identity, and protocol bridging.

strength_areas.description: Amount of information (47), Scalability (49), Function efficiency (40), and Controllability (48) together span the key ML tradeoffs: data volume vs training cost, model size vs inference latency, accuracy vs explainability, and hyperparameter control vs automation.
strength_areas.example_contradiction: Improving Amount of information (47) worsens Function efficiency (40): explore data distillation, curriculum learning, and active sampling as inventive strategies.

llm_generation_assessment.summary: The entire matrix — including the nominally 'classic' 1-39 region — was effectively LLM-generated. The 17.6% match rate against Altshuller's patent-derived data means 82.4% of classic-region cells contain principle recommendations that differ from the empirical original. This is not a minor calibration issue; it is a fundamental data provenance problem.
when_llm_data_is_useful: Brainstorming and ideation: LLM-generated cells can serve as creative prompts that expand the solution space, even if they lack empirical grounding. Treating them as 'suggestions worth exploring' rather than 'validated solutions' is appropriate.

Modern domain parameters (40-50): Since no patent-derived data exists for these parameters, LLM-generated cells are the best available starting point. They encode plausible analogical reasoning about how inventive principles might apply to security, scalability, sustainability, etc.

Cross-domain exploration: When mapping physical-engineering principles to software or organizational problems, the LLM's tendency to generalize may actually help bridge conceptual gaps that literal patent data cannot.

Rapid prototyping of contradiction analyses: When speed matters more than rigor, the full 50x50 matrix provides fast lookup without needing to assess whether a parameter falls in the classic or extended region.
Educational contexts: For teaching TRIZ methodology, the extended matrix demonstrates how the framework can be adapted — with explicit caveats about data quality.
when_llm_data_is_dangerous: Safety-critical engineering decisions: Using unvalidated principle recommendations for structural, medical, chemical, or aerospace design contradictions could lead to overlooking proven solutions or pursuing unsound ones.

Replacing empirical Altshuller data: The classic 39x39 region of this matrix should NOT be used as a substitute for the canonical matrix. The 82.4% divergence means users are getting different (and unvalidated) recommendations for well-studied contradictions.

Authoritative reference: Citing this matrix as 'TRIZ-based' without disclosing its LLM-generated provenance is misleading. The inventive principles are genuine Altshuller, but the cell assignments are not.

Systematic innovation processes: In structured TRIZ workflows (e.g., ARIZ, patent circumvention), cell accuracy matters. LLM-generated cells may suggest principles that are plausible-sounding but miss the specific empirical patterns Altshuller found in patent analysis.
Benchmarking or academic research: Studies comparing TRIZ effectiveness or principle utilization patterns would produce misleading results if based on this matrix's classic-region data.
risk_mitigation: Always cross-reference the classic 39x39 region against altshuller_39x39.json before relying on any cell
Treat all cells as hypothesis-generating, not prescriptive
Label outputs clearly as 'extended TRIZ (LLM-seeded)' to maintain intellectual honesty
Validate principle recommendations against domain expertise before implementation
Use the extended region (40-50) as a structured brainstorming aid, not as an engineering standard

when_to_use.summary: Use this matrix when the problem involves modern technology parameters (40-50) that classic TRIZ does not cover, or when you need a single unified lookup across both classic and modern domains.

recommended_scenarios.rationale: Classic TRIZ has no parameters for data volume, scalability, or algorithmic efficiency. This matrix enables direct contradiction analysis for ML pipeline design: e.g., improving model accuracy (Amount of information, 47) while controlling inference latency (Speed, 9).

Security (44) and Compatibility (43) are absent from classic TRIZ. This matrix provides inventive principle suggestions for the ubiquitous security-vs-usability and security-vs-interoperability contradictions.

recommended_scenarios.scenario: Sustainable product design and circular economy
recommended_scenarios.rationale: Environmental impact (46) and Harmful emissions (42) enable explicit sustainability contradiction modeling. E.g., reducing lifecycle emissions while maintaining product durability and cost competitiveness.

Aesthetics (50) and Controllability (48) fill critical gaps for interaction design. The matrix can suggest inventive approaches to contradictions like 'increase information density without cluttering the interface.'

Scalability (49) directly addresses horizontal/vertical scaling contradictions. Combined with Compatibility (43) and Reliability (27), it covers microservice decomposition, fault tolerance, and API versioning tradeoffs.
Safety (45) as a first-class parameter enables contradiction modeling for fail-safe design, sensor redundancy vs weight/cost, and response time vs safety verification overhead.

recommended_scenarios.scenario: IoT and edge computing device design
recommended_scenarios.rationale: IoT devices face unique multi-parameter contradictions: security with constrained resources, compatibility across protocols, scalability of fleet management, and energy efficiency. Extended parameters address all of these.

When the goal is creative exploration rather than rigorous engineering, the full 50-parameter space encourages participants to consider contradictions they might not have framed otherwise. The LLM-generated nature is acceptable in a brainstorming context.

when_not_to_use.summary: Do not use this matrix when authoritative, patent-derived TRIZ data is required — particularly for the classic 39-parameter domain. The 17.6% match rate against Altshuller makes the classic region unreliable as a primary reference.

contraindicated_scenarios.rationale: For contradictions involving only parameters 1-39 (weight, strength, speed, shape, etc.), the canonical altshuller_39x39.json is far more authoritative. The triz_ai matrix's classic cells diverge from patent-derived data in 82.4% of cases.

Patent-around strategies require the specific inventive principles that Altshuller extracted from actual patent analysis. LLM-generated principle assignments may suggest approaches that are plausible but not grounded in real patent patterns.
In aerospace, medical, nuclear, or chemical engineering, contradiction resolution recommendations must be traceable to validated sources. LLM-generated cells lack this traceability.
contraindicated_scenarios.use_instead: altshuller_39x39.json for classic params; domain-specific matrices (e.g., drug_safety_18x18.json) for specialized domains

contraindicated_scenarios.rationale: Studies on TRIZ effectiveness, principle distribution, or contradiction patterns will produce misleading results if based on LLM-generated data presented as empirical. Researchers should use canonical sources.

Students should learn the canonical matrix first. Introducing LLM-generated variants without clear provenance labeling creates confusion about what TRIZ actually prescribes vs what an AI model interpolated.
If using TRIZ to justify design decisions in a regulatory context, the data must be traceable to recognized sources. LLM-generated matrices have no recognized standing.

ideal_approach.summary: Use a hybrid strategy: leverage the triz_ai 50x50 matrix for its EXTENDED region (parameters 40-50) where no empirical alternative exists, but always cross-reference the classic region (parameters 1-39) against the canonical altshuller_39x39.json.

step_1.detail: Determine whether the improving and worsening parameters both fall in the classic range (1-39), the extended range (40-50), or span both regions.

step_2_classic_only.action: Use altshuller_39x39.json as the primary source
step_2_classic_only.fallback: Optionally consult triz_ai_50x50 as a secondary brainstorming input, but flag that its recommendations differ from canonical in 82.4% of cases.

step_2_extended_involved.condition: One or both parameters are in range 40-50
step_2_extended_involved.action: Use triz_ai_50x50.json — it is the only matrix in this collection that covers these parameters
step_2_extended_involved.caveat: Treat recommendations as LLM-generated hypotheses. Validate against domain expertise. Consider also consulting mann_matrix2003_48x48.json which covers some overlapping extended parameters (if populated).

step_3.action: Apply inventive principles with appropriate confidence calibration
step_3.detail: For patent-derived cells (from altshuller_39x39): treat as empirically supported starting points. For LLM-generated cells (from triz_ai_50x50): treat as plausible analogical suggestions that require validation.

step_4.detail: When recommending inventive principles to stakeholders, clearly indicate whether the recommendation comes from canonical Altshuller data or LLM-generated extensions. This maintains intellectual honesty and calibrates stakeholder expectations.

matrix_selection_decision_tree.both_params_40_to_50: triz_ai_50x50.json (only available source; 67 cells in this region)
matrix_selection_decision_tree.domain_specific_need: Check drug_safety_18x18.json, biotriz_24x24.json, healthcare_servqual.json for specialized domains before falling back to general matrices

extended_region_principle_frequency_top_.name: Mechanics substitution
extended_region_principle_frequency_top_.note: Heavily overrepresented vs classic TRIZ; LLM bias toward 'replace mechanism with field/signal' abstraction

extended_region_principle_frequency_top_.name: Feedback
extended_region_principle_frequency_top_.note: Second most frequent — reflects modern control-systems thinking in LLM training data

extended_region_principle_frequency_top_.name: Dynamics
extended_region_principle_frequency_top_.note: Reasonable: adaptability is central to modern system concerns

extended_region_principle_frequency_top_.name: Inert atmosphere
extended_region_principle_frequency_top_.note: Suspiciously high for extended params; possible LLM over-generalization of 'isolation' concept

extended_region_principle_frequency_top_.name: Flexible shells and thin films
extended_region_principle_frequency_top_.note: Likely mapped by LLM as 'abstraction layer' or 'wrapper' for software contexts

extended_region_principle_frequency_top_.name: Copying
extended_region_principle_frequency_top_.note: Maps well to caching, replication, and redundancy in modern systems

extended_region_principle_frequency_top_.name: Periodic action
extended_region_principle_frequency_top_.note: Batch processing, polling, heartbeats in distributed systems

notable_distribution_anomalies: Principle 28 (Mechanics substitution) is the #1 principle in the extended region but only #3 in the classic region — the LLM appears to over-apply the 'replace physical mechanism with field/signal' abstraction to modern domains

Principle 23 (Feedback) is #2 in the extended region but only #35 in canonical Altshuller — a massive over-representation suggesting the LLM projects modern control-theory thinking onto all extended parameters
Principle 35 (Parameter changes) drops from #1 in the classic region to #11 in extended — the LLM underweights the most empirically frequent classic principle
Principle 39 (Inert atmosphere) at rank #5 in extended is anomalous — likely the LLM over-generalizes 'isolation/containment' metaphors

vs_altshuller_39x39.overlap: Parameters 1-39 nominally identical, but cell data diverges at 82.4%
vs_altshuller_39x39.disadvantage: Classic region data is unreliable; cannot serve as Altshuller substitute

vs_mann_matrix2003_48x48.overlap: Both extend beyond 39 parameters; Mann adds params 40-48 covering some similar concepts
vs_mann_matrix2003_48x48.advantage: triz_ai adds Scalability (49) and Aesthetics (50); has populated cells (Mann's matrix in this collection has 0 populated cells)
vs_mann_matrix2003_48x48.disadvantage: Mann's matrix was developed by TRIZ practitioners with domain expertise; triz_ai's was LLM-generated

vs_domain_specific_matrices.overlap: drug_safety_18x18 and healthcare_servqual cover specialized domains that params 44-46 partially address
vs_domain_specific_matrices.advantage: triz_ai provides a single general-purpose matrix; domain-specific matrices require identifying the right specialized matrix
vs_domain_specific_matrices.disadvantage: Domain-specific matrices were developed by domain experts with validated parameters and principles
